summary: Full backend + frontend e2e testing for TechnoDonate. All 10 backend pytest cases pass and all critical frontend flows (Submit page validation/profanity/cooldown/toast and Display page login/hero rotation/TTS toggle/reset/logout) work end-to-end against the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL.

minor.issue: Pydantic field_validator strips whitespace AFTER min_length validation, so a payload with whitespace-only nickname (e.g. '   ') currently passes Pydantic (min_length=1 is on the raw string) and saves an empty-after-strip nickname. Consider running strip in a model_validator(mode='before') so trimmed values are re-validated against min_length=1.

Sort key 'timestamp' is a string (ISO-8601). It happens to sort correctly because ISO strings are lexicographically ordered, but storing as native BSON Date would be more robust against locale/timezone edge cases.

issues: With ttsOn=true the TTS button auto-speaks the hero message every 6s via window.speechSynthesis. In a headless test/browser without speech support this is silent, but on real devices it could be noisy on first load — consider defaulting ttsOn=false (or persisting last choice in sessionStorage).
Button text uses Unicode ellipsis '…' which is intentional per problem statement — no issue, just confirming.

action_items: (Optional) Strengthen nickname/message Pydantic validation so whitespace-only inputs are rejected at API level (currently relies on frontend trim).
(Optional) Default TTS off on Display to avoid surprising audio on first load.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py min_length=1 is evaluated before the field_validator strip; whitespace-only strings get through validation and are then trimmed to empty before insert. Use model_validator(mode='before') or validator with always=True to strip first.

Submit.jsx useEffect dependency '[cooldown > 0]' uses a boolean expression as a dependency entry — works but linter-unfriendly. Prefer storing the boolean in a derived const inside the effect or use 'cooldown' directly.
Display.jsx archive computation uses messages.slice(0, heroIdx).slice(-8).reverse() — fine for small lists; OK.
All interactive elements correctly expose data-testid attributes — good coverage.
test_credentials: Display login email=xnytxs@gmail.com password=h6h6h678 — verified working
seed_data_creation: Tests create messages with 'TEST_' / 'PolledNick' prefixes and clean up via DELETE /api/messages before/after each backend test and after frontend display tests (reset flow).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_messages.py covers create/list/count/delete/validation and runs in ~3s. Frontend public URL: https://cinema-donate-queue.preview.emergentagent.com. Display creds: xnytxs@gmail.com / h6h6h678. Hero rotation interval is 6s; polling interval 1500ms. data-testid coverage is comprehensive. No mocked APIs.
rca of the issue: N/A — no blocking issues found. Minor backend validation gap (whitespace-only strings) is documented in action_items.
